name: Beach house style studio
subtitle: Colorful house in the hills of Tenerife
tagline: Your cosy mountain retreat in Valleseco
welcomeMessage: Welcome to my colourful house in the hills of Tenerife! Nestled in the charming village of Valleseco, it is full of character and surrounded by nature. I hope you love it as much as I do. Reach out anytime if you need anything at all.

address.display: Calle Manuel Ravina 69, Valleseco, Santa Cruz de Tenerife
address.nearestLandmark: Near Punto y Coma Minimarket, Valleseco village
address.addressNote: Google Maps is not 100% accurate for the last stretch, so please use the photo guide!

wifi.notes: If you experience connection issues, please let me know so I can help.

checkin.keyLocation: Look for the white door on the lower part of the house. The keybox is right next to it.
steps: Park at the recommended parking area (there is always space and you won't have to climb almost any stairs)
Starting from the parking, move in this direction and walk until the street ends, then go down the stairs on the left side
Go down to the next street below and turn right (this is the street of the house)
Walk straight, cross the garden with cacti and other plants, then continue between the houses
After 1 min, on the left-hand side, you will see a white-coloured house
Your apartment is in the lower part of the house. Look for the white door opening from the stairs at the side of the house, with a key box next to it
checkin.notes: If you have any trouble finding the house, call or WhatsApp me anytime and I am happy to guide you step by step. Note: Google Maps is not 100% accurate for the last stretch, so please follow the photo guide!

gettingHere.byCar: The best is to park at the recommended spot (link below). There are always spaces available and you won't have to climb almost any stairs. Then follow the walking directions in the photo guide. Google Maps is not 100% accurate for the last stretch, so please use the photo guide!
gettingHere.parking: There are always parking spaces available and it is a short flat walk to the house entrance.
gettingHere.publicTransport: There are various buses from Santa Cruz de Tenerife (e.g. Nr. 909, 910, 916). Make sure to get off at Valleseco (it's a small village with just one stop). Once you get off, find the Punto y Coma Minimarket and take the stairs on the left about 20 metres after passing it. It's a 5-10 minute uphill walk to the house. Google Maps is not fully accurate for the last stretch, so please follow the photo guide!

directionPhotos.caption: Park up here. There are always spaces available and you won't have to climb almost any stairs.
Starting from the parking, move in this direction and walk until the street ends, then go down the stairs on the left side.
Go down to the next street below and turn right. This is the street of the house.
Walk straight and cross the garden with cacti and other plants.
After 1 min, on the left-hand side, you will see a white-coloured house.
Getting closer. Your apartment is in the lower part of the house.
Look for the white door opening from the stairs at the side of the house, with a key box next to it. Code: 6015

gettingHere.taxi: Taxis are available from Tenerife North (TFN) airport and from Santa Cruz (costs about 10 EUR). Best is to agree on the fare before departing or ensure the meter is running. From Tenerife South, I recommend to take the bus to Santa Cruz first as it's further away. Ask the taxi driver to drop you at the parking spot linked above in the "By Car" section, then follow the step-by-step photo guide from photo 1.

directionPhotosBus.caption: Find the Punto y Coma Minimarket. After passing it, take the stairs on the left to go up.
Find the stairs on the left, about 20 metres after passing the minimarket.
After 5-10 minutes of climbing (depending on your ambitions!), you will see the house.
It's the lower part of the house. Look for the white door opening from the stairs at the side, with a key box next to it. Code: 6015

houseRules: Please smoke outside only. The outdoor areas are all yours, but kindly keep the inside of the house smoke-free. Thank you!
Please keep it peaceful. Quiet hours are 11pm to 8am. No parties or events
Please treat the apartment as your own, and let me know right away if anything gets damaged. I understand accidents happen

appliances.name: Electricity
appliances.instructions: The house's electrical system is a little old, so it can't handle several high-power appliances running at once: for example a hair dryer, the oven and the stove. To be on the safe side, please use just one of these at a time. If the electricity ever cuts out, don't worry at all, just shut down all appliances and switch back the main electrical fuse (next to the entrance door)!

appliances.name: Kitchen
appliances.instructions: You have a kitchen area equipped with an electric stove, a mini oven, a fridge as well as the normal kitchen tools you might need. ⚠️ Important: as it's always summer in Tenerife, bugs and insects never sleep. Please make sure to remove all leftover food and throw it in the bin right away, and I also recommend cleaning the dishes right after eating to avoid flies and other insects showing up in the kitchen.

appliances.name: Washing Machine
appliances.instructions: You'll find the washing machine in the hall area, feel free to use it! The fast programme called "Rapido" is usually all you need. If you have any questions, just let me know.

appliances.name: Drinking Water
appliances.instructions: Tap water in Tenerife is said to be safe and clean, though most people prefer bottled water, which you'll find at any supermarket or kiosk. The cheaper and more practical option is to grab a 5L, 8L or larger gallon.

appliances.name: Bathroom
appliances.instructions: You'll find fresh towels, a hair dryer, and soap and shampoo all ready for you. If anything else is missing, just let me know and I'll sort it out.

appliances.name: Bathroom Ventilation
appliances.instructions: ⚠️ IMPORTANT: As the bathroom doesn't have a window, it's important to get the steam out. There's a ventilation system that should ideally be on for 20 minutes every day. It works together with the bathroom's light, so please leave it on for a while after you use the bathroom, especially if you take a shower. Also, please make sure to leave the door open when you're not using the bathroom.

appliances.name: Plants
appliances.instructions: If you're staying a few days, the plants would love a little water every 3-4 days. They'll appreciate it!

appliances.name: Mari, the Lovely Neighbour
appliances.instructions: My right hand and greatest help is the lovely Mari, who lives right next door. If anything comes up at all (say you need an extra towel), she's always happy to help. She might even pop by with some homemade sweets if she has the time! Mari speaks only Spanish, but don't worry if you don't, somehow it always works out beautifully. 🙂

items.name: Playa de Valleseco
items.tip: The closest beach to the house, only around a 5 minute walk down the stairs. It is a new beach with all basic amenities and a very easy option for a quick swim close to home.

items.name: Playa de las Teresitas
items.tip: Playa de las Teresitas is an artificial golden sandy beach just north of San Andrés. It is long, beautiful, and safe for swimming or paddle boarding, although it can get windy. The white sand was imported from the Sahara, which makes it very different from the usual black sand beaches in Tenerife. It is around 7 minutes by car from the house, or around 15 minutes by bus.

items.name: Boca Cangrejo
items.tip: Boca Cangrejo is a small and very charming Canarian village with a little beach, traditional fishing boats, white houses, nice local bars, and a great restaurant for fresh fish and paella. Look for the bay decorated with colourful hearts and paintings made by a local fisherman.

items.name: Playa Montaña Roja
items.tip: Playa Montaña Roja is a long, calm, very beautiful beach near El Médano. It is especially nice for sunset.

items.name: Playa de Bollullo
items.tip: Playa de Bollullo is a gorgeous natural beach in the scenic La Orotava Valley in the north of Tenerife. The drive there is a little adventure itself, but the beach is very much worth it.

items.name: Playa de Benijo
items.tip: Playa de Benijo is an epic rocky beach in the north of the island. The drive there is very scenic and already part of the experience.

items.name: San Andrés village
items.tip: For eating out close to the house, go to San Andrés or Santa Cruz downtown. Las Teresitas has small beach bars for drinks and tapas, but for a proper meal, it is better to go into San Andrés village.

items.name: Tasca El Asunto Los Parres
items.tip: A small family run restaurant with Canarian wines, which are quite rare to find, and amazing food. They also have vegetarian and vegan options.

items.name: Lena
items.tip: Great Canarian food, a long menu, and very nice staff. It is not the cheapest option, but the food is really good.

items.name: Kiosco in Boca Cangrejo
items.tip: A good option in Boca Cangrejo for fresh fish and very good paella.

items.name: Aroma y Sabor
items.tip: A great restaurant recommendation for the way back from Teide. It serves Peruvian food, is super delicious, and offers good food for a good price.

items.name: San Andrés
items.tip: San Andrés is one of the oldest villages of the Canary Islands, founded around 1497. It is close to Las Teresitas and sits beautifully next to the Anaga mountains.

items.name: Boca Cangrejo
items.tip: A small, charming Canarian fishing village with white houses, traditional boats, a little beach, local bars, and a bay decorated with colourful hearts and paintings.

items.name: El Médano
items.tip: El Médano is a small resort town on the south east coast, close to the south airport. It is very laid back, casual, clean, and typically Spanish in look and feel. It is popular with windsurfers, kitesurfers, sporty people, and digital nomads. It is a great place to walk around, swim, do watersports, check out the weekend market, eat out, or go for drinks.

items.name: Los Cristianos and Playa de las Américas
items.tip: These are the most famous touristic spots in the south, with the safest weather, usually sunny and warm. They are busy and very touristic, with hotels, shops, clubs, services, diving clubs, and everything you may need.

items.name: Puerto de la Cruz
items.tip: Puerto de la Cruz is a very historical and authentic Canarian town with typical houses, museums, and nice little beaches. It is definitely worth a visit.

items.name: Los Gigantes
items.tip: Los Gigantes is a resort town on the west coast of Tenerife. It is famous for the giant rock formations, Acantilados de Los Gigantes, which rise from the sea to a height of around 500 to 800 metres. The town is nice to walk around, although it is a little touristic.

items.name: Masca
items.tip: Masca is one of the most beautiful villages on the island, and maybe even in the Canary Islands. Go there, walk around, enjoy an almond cake, try the tropical fruit juices, and maybe do a hike. Stay for sunset if you have the chance.

items.name: Anaga area
items.tip: The Anaga area is the closest and greenest mountain area to the house. It has many hiking paths and small villages.

items.name: Teide National Park
items.tip: Teide National Park is the national park around Mount Teide, with many different treks and volcanic landscapes.

items.name: Mount Teide
items.tip: Mount Teide is a volcano and the highest point in Spain at 3,715 metres. You can drive or take the bus up to around 2,200 metres, then hike up or take the cable car. The hike takes around 4 to 5 hours depending on your fitness level. For the last 50 metres to the summit, you need to register in advance. Even without that final part, the view is amazing and rewarding. It is not an easy hike and it can get cold up there, but it is very much worth it.

items.name: Orotava area
items.tip: The Orotava area is a wine region on the northern side of the island and offers many nice treks and scenic routes.

items.name: Barranco de Masca
items.tip: If you visit Masca and like hiking, Barranco de Masca is a great option to consider.

items.name: Komoot app
items.tip: Tip: Use the Komoot app. It is very accurate and has lots of recorded hikes for Tenerife.

items.name: Tenerife wine tasting
items.tip: Tenerife has some of the highest elevation vineyards in Europe. The production is limited and the wines taste different here because of the volcanic soil and climate. The best way to experience local wine is to arrange a visit and tasting with one of the bodegas.

items.name: Bodegas Monje Tenerife in El Sauzal
items.tip: Recommended bodega in El Sauzal.

items.name: Bodega El Lomo
items.tip: Recommended for a wine visit and tasting.

items.name: Bodegas Suertes del Marqués
items.tip: Another recommended local winery experience.

items.name: Siam Park
items.tip: Siam Park is one of the most famous water parks in the world, with many attractions and slides.

items.name: Loro Parque
items.tip: Loro Parque is a modern zoo with many different types of rescued animals.

items.name: El Médano watersports
items.tip: El Médano is a great place for windsurfing, kitesurfing, swimming, beach walks, and a relaxed sporty atmosphere.

items.name: Los Cristianos and Playa de las Américas
items.tip: A good area for tourist services, diving clubs, nightlife, shopping, restaurants, and the most reliable sunny weather.

items.name: Mini supermarkets in Valleseco
items.tip: There are two small mini supermarkets in the village where you can buy basic things like water and simple groceries.

items.name: Pharmacy
items.tip: There is also a pharmacy very close to the main road. Please check Google Maps for current opening hours.

items.name: HiperDino
items.distance: 20 min walk or 5 min by car
items.tip: For bigger grocery shopping, HiperDino toward Santa Cruz is a good option. It is around 20 minutes on foot or 5 minutes by car. It has a wide range of products, although it can be a little pricey.

items.name: Santa Cruz Market Hall
items.tip: A good option for fresh food, local products, and a more local shopping experience.

items.name: Lidl
items.tip: Lidl is further away, but useful for larger grocery shopping.

items.name: Mercadona or Spar
items.tip: Mercadona and Spar are also further away, but good for bigger shopping. Google Maps is accurate for locations and opening times.

eatAndDrink.name: Karlina Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Easiest no-car dinner option near Avlida. Ranked among the top restaurants near Tombs of the Kings.

eatAndDrink.name: Dias Zeus Greek Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Reliable nearby option on Tombs of the Kings Avenue. Greek and Mediterranean with strong guest ratings.

eatAndDrink.name: Tea for Two
eatAndDrink.tip: Good for an easy breakfast or lunch close to home. Friendly service and great value.

eatAndDrink.name: Psarou Fish Tavern
eatAndDrink.tip: Cozy tavern known for fresh fish, traditional meze, and a friendly local vibe.

eatAndDrink.name: Kingfisher Taverna
eatAndDrink.tip: Small, relaxed taverna serving high-quality fish dishes and local favourites in a more upscale setting.

eatAndDrink.name: Three Little Pigs
eatAndDrink.tip: Local favourite with high-quality food, great service, and a lovely atmosphere. Their house wine is a treat!

eatAndDrink.name: Kings Avenue Mall Food Court
eatAndDrink.tip: Not special, but very practical. Good for families, quick food, coffee, or escaping the heat.

eatAndDrink.name: Old Town Paphos cafés
eatAndDrink.tip: More local feeling than the harbour. Nice for brunch, coffee, or early evening drinks.

eatAndDrink.name: Pallia Ilektriki
eatAndDrink.tip: A good Old Town option. Ranked among popular places near Old Town Paphos.

eatAndDrink.name: Ta Pinakia
eatAndDrink.tip: Good if you want something less harbour-focused. Well-rated near Old Town Paphos.

eatAndDrink.name: O Firis
eatAndDrink.tip: Modern twist on Cypriot cuisine with generous portions. Live music almost every evening. Book ahead!

eatAndDrink.name: Paphos Harbour restaurants
eatAndDrink.tip: Touristy but reliable. Many options for dinner, drinks, and a sunset walk by the sea.

eatAndDrink.name: King's Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Popular harbour area choice. Good fish and Cypriot food.

eatAndDrink.name: Theo's Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Good for seafood near the harbour. Sea bass, octopus, prawns, and calamari.

eatAndDrink.name: The Moorings Café Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: More about the harbour atmosphere than fine dining. Easy and relaxed.

eatAndDrink.name: West End Hotel & Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Family-run with generous portions and delicious seafood, steps from the sea.

eatAndDrink.name: Oniro by the Sea
eatAndDrink.tip: One of the best scenic spots in the Paphos area. Combine with Sea Caves and Edro III shipwreck.

eatAndDrink.name: Kathikas wine village restaurants
eatAndDrink.tip: More authentic than the seafront. Best for a longer lunch or early dinner with wine tasting.

eatAndDrink.name: Latchi Harbour tavernas
eatAndDrink.tip: Best combined with a Blue Lagoon boat trip, Baths of Aphrodite, or a Polis day trip.

eatAndDrink.name: Lidl
eatAndDrink.tip: Budget-friendly with a wide range of groceries. Great for stocking up on basics.

eatAndDrink.name: AlphaMega
eatAndDrink.tip: Large supermarket with a wide variety of fresh produce and local Cypriot items.

shopping.name: Kings Avenue Mall
shopping.tip: The main shopping mall in Paphos. Wide range of shops, restaurants and a cinema.

shopping.name: Paphos Market
shopping.tip: Covered marketplace with stalls for clothing, bags, jewelry, beach gear and local souvenirs.

shopping.name: Old Town
shopping.tip: Wander through the beautiful Old Town and discover many small boutique stores and artisan shops.

gettingAround.name: Bus
gettingAround.info: To explore Tenerife by public transport, start by checking the TITSA website or the TITSA app. From Valleseco, the easiest option is usually to travel first to Intercambiador Santa Cruz, the main bus station in Santa Cruz. From there, you can walk into the city centre, take the tram to La Laguna, or change to buses going to other towns, airports, beaches, and the south of the island. For Playa de Las Teresitas, take TITSA line 910 towards San Andrés / Playa de Las Teresitas. You can either catch the bus from the Valleseco area when available, or first go to Intercambiador Santa Cruz and change there. Before leaving, I recommend checking the exact route on Google Maps, TITSA, or the TITSA app, as bus times can change depending on the day, season, and public holidays.

contacts.name: Emergency Services
contacts.note: Police, Ambulance, Fire. Free from any phone

contacts.name: Hospital Universitario Nuestra Señora de Candelaria
contacts.note: Main hospital in Santa Cruz de Tenerife

emergency.nearestHospital: Hospital Universitario Nuestra Señora de Candelaria, Ctra. Gral. del Rosario 145, Santa Cruz de Tenerife.
emergency.nearestPharmacy: Several pharmacies within walking distance on the main road. ask me if you need a recommendation.

steps: If you could give any dishes a quick wash and leave them on the drying rack, that would be great!
If you could clear out the fridge and pop any leftover food in the bin, I'd appreciate it!

steps.text: It would be great if you dispose of the garbage. You will find big containers just before you leave the village.

steps: Before heading off, please lock the windows and the door.
Please switch off all the lights and any electric appliances (oven, stove, blender) when you're done.
When you're ready to go, please lock up and return all keys to the keybox, resetting the code after.
Please drop me a WhatsApp or Airbnb message to let me know you've left and I'd truly love a review if you enjoyed your stay! 🩵
